title: Research: Herbalism Epistemology
content:
## Context
The core tension: what counts as evidence in herbalism?

## Research Questions
- How do herbalists think about evidence? (not just 'tradition says' but what makes tradition credible?)
- What's the case for traditional use as evidence? (convergent traditions, long observation periods, etc.)
- What are the failure modes? (survivorship bias, placebo, nocebo, regression to mean)
- How do we weight different evidence types? (RCT > cohort > case study > traditional > mechanistic > in vitro)

## Relevance to Folk Protocol
This is the intellectual foundation. We need a clear, defensible epistemology that's not 'only RCTs count' but also not 'grandma said so.'

## Inspiration
Slime Mold Time Mold's approach: take weird hypotheses seriously, run citizen science, be transparent about uncertainty.

## Output
Position paper on Folk Protocol's evidence philosophy. Could become an 'About' page section.
